body:
my suggestion was to let them bring it here at their risk

----- Original Message ----

Subject: RE:

FOB St.
  Thomas means putting the boat on one of those big boat
carriers. It will cost in excess of $100,000 and the projected delivery dates
here were as late as next February. We can insure the boat and bring it here
for a lot less money than $100,000. 
 

  
 










From: J. Epstein
[mailto:jeeproject@yahoo.com] 

Sent: Monday, October 23, 2006
4:13 PM

To: John Amerling

Subject: Re:
 




  
 





why not propose a cost
fob st thomas
 



----- Original Message
----

From: John Amerling <jamerling@rrcaribbean.com>

To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>

Cc: Darren Indyke <dkiesq@aol.com>

Sent: Monday, October 23, 2006 3:52:33 PM

Subject: 
 



The boat in Tortola is
aluminum. It has a quirky owner who was asking $600K for it before he decided
to change out the engines and ask for more. The barges in
 Florida are both more expensive and require
repairs. 
 

 
 

As to the Tortola 
possibility I have waited out enough West Indian pipe dreams to recommend that
this one not be listened to. The price will be too high and as soon as we
target the boat we end up in a seamless web of negotiations which go no where.
 

 
 

I recommend that we move ahead with the Chateau Thierry.
Three pictures are attached. It is in Southampton
 England . The price, including broker’s
commission is $275,000. The offer would be subject to a Bosco visit and if that
works out ok, his engagement of a surveyor which would still have to be ok
before a commitment would be made. The barge appears to be ready to sail and
can be brought back under its own power to the northeast where it could be
brought down the inland waterway. If we wanted to make any changes to it, that
work could be lined up in the US 
before it is brought here.

FOB St. Thomas means putting the boat on one of those big boat carriers. It will cost in excess of $100,000 and the projected delivery dates here were as late as next February. We can insure the boat and bring it here for a lot less money than $100,000.

 

From: J. Epstein [mailto:jeeproject@yahoo.com]

why not propose a cost fob st thomas

----- Original Message ----
From: John Amerling <jamerling@rrcaribbean.com>
To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Cc: Darren Indyke <dkiesq@aol.com>
Sent: Monday, October 23, 2006 3:52:33 PM
Subject:

The boat in Tortola is aluminum. It has a quirky owner who was asking $600K for it before he decided to change out the engines and ask for more. The barges in Florida are both more expensive and require repairs.

 

As to the Tortola possibility I have waited out enough West Indian pipe dreams to recommend that this one not be listened to. The price will be too high and as soon as we target the boat we end up in a seamless web of negotiations which go no where.

 

I recommend that we move ahead with the Chateau Thierry. Three pictures are attached. It is in Southampton England . The price, including broker’s commission is $275,000. The offer would be subject to a Bosco visit and if that works out ok, his engagement of a surveyor which would still have to be ok before a commitment would be made. The barge appears to be ready to sail and can be brought back under its own power to the northeast where it could be brought down the inland waterway. If we wanted to make any changes to it, that work could be lined up in the US before it is brought here.
